title: lichen-ipv6 BufferTooSmall uses needed/available while lichen-core uses required/provided
description:
Two different field naming conventions for the same concept:

lichen-ipv6/src/lib.rs Ipv6Error:
  BufferTooSmall { needed: usize, available: usize }

lichen-core/src/error.rs BufferTooSmall:
  pub struct BufferTooSmall {
      pub required: usize,
      pub provided: usize,
  }

lichen-core/src/ipv6.rs uses:
  BufferTooSmall(BufferTooSmall)  // wraps the struct

What goes wrong: Code handling buffer errors must use different field names depending on which crate's error type it receives. If these are ever unified, one set of callers must update their field access.
close_reason: Already fixed - both lichen-ipv6 and lichen-core use required/provided field names
